original_text: Colorado legalizes psilocybin mushrooms for therapeutic use.
identified_property: comprehensiveness and accessibility of the therapeutic framework

variations.label: least_preferable
variations.text: Colorado legalizes psilocybin mushrooms for therapeutic use under a highly restrictive framework limited to a single licensed facility, with no insurance coverage, prohibitive out-of-pocket costs, and eligibility confined to a narrow set of terminal diagnoses.

variations.label: low
variations.text: Colorado legalizes psilocybin mushrooms for therapeutic use under a restrictive framework with few licensed facilities concentrated in Denver, lengthy approval processes, no insurance coverage, and eligibility limited to treatment-resistant depression and PTSD.

variations.label: below_midpoint
variations.text: Colorado legalizes psilocybin mushrooms for therapeutic use under a moderately accessible framework with licensed facilities in several urban areas, a streamlined but still multi-step approval process, limited sliding-scale fee options, and eligibility covering a modest range of mental health conditions.

variations.label: midpoint
variations.text: Colorado legalizes psilocybin mushrooms for therapeutic use.

variations.label: above_midpoint
variations.text: Colorado legalizes psilocybin mushrooms for therapeutic use under a well-developed framework with licensed facilities across urban and suburban regions, partial insurance coverage, standardized practitioner training, and eligibility encompassing a broad range of mental health and substance-use conditions.

variations.label: high
variations.text: Colorado legalizes psilocybin mushrooms for therapeutic use under a comprehensive framework with licensed facilities in every county including rural areas, robust insurance coverage, fully standardized practitioner certification, public education campaigns, and eligibility open to most mental health, substance-use, and existential-distress conditions.

variations.label: most_preferable
variations.text: Colorado legalizes psilocybin mushrooms for therapeutic use under a maximally comprehensive and accessible framework featuring licensed facilities in every community including mobile and telehealth-integrated services, full insurance and Medicaid coverage, rigorous and widely available practitioner training, culturally competent care standards, ongoing publicly funded research, and eligibility open to any individual who may therapeutically benefit.

quality_checks.reasoning: Each tier progressively expands the quality of the therapeutic framework along multiple dimensions—geographic reach of facilities, financial accessibility (insurance coverage), breadth of eligible conditions, practitioner standards, and supportive infrastructure—without changing the core scenario of Colorado legalizing psilocybin for therapeutic use. The distinctions are meaningful at each step: Tier 1 is extremely narrow and costly; Tier 4 is the unadorned original; Tier 7 represents the most inclusive, well-resourced, and equitable system possible.

preference_direction: Tier 7 is most preferable because a maximally comprehensive and accessible therapeutic framework ensures the greatest number of people can benefit safely and equitably from psilocybin-assisted therapy, with robust training, broad coverage, cultural competence, and ongoing research supporting the best possible outcomes.
